items.q: Which Spanish 'you' do you use with a stranger or elderly person?
items.why: Students use tú with everyone like English 'you'. Spanish distinguishes FORMAL (usted) for politeness/distance and INFORMAL (tú) for friends/family. Usted takes third-person verb forms, which trips students up.

A notorious faux ami that causes real confusion. 'Estoy embarazada' = 'I am pregnant'. For 'embarrassed' use 'avergonzado/a'. Native speakers laugh at this one - learners avoid by association with shame.

items.q: How do you say 'I'm going to the beach' (talking to someone elsewhere) in Spanish?
items.why: Students confuse ir (to go) and venir (to come). IR = movement AWAY from speaker; VENIR = movement TOWARDS speaker. 'I'm going there' = voy; 'I'm coming here' = vengo. Critical for holidays/travel vocab.

items.q: Who led the conquest of the Aztecs?
items.why: It's Hernan Cortes. “Francisco Pizarro” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Who wrote El laberinto de la soledad?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Octavio Paz. “Carlos Fuentes” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: In what year did Columbus arrive in America?
items.why: The real figure is 1492. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
